message:
fix(storage): preserve thread spawn failures as actionable diagnostics

Replace the unreachable!() thread-spawn fallbacks in the storage workers
with panic messages that keep the underlying OS error text intact.

Why this change matters:
- Thread creation can fail in real environments because of resource
  exhaustion, ulimit constraints, or process-level limits.
- Treating those paths as unreachable converts a diagnosable startup
  failure into a misleading panic with no useful cause information.
- The write-behind queue and commit coalescer are critical background
  workers, so when they fail to start the operator needs the exact worker
  name and spawn error immediately.

What changed:
- Annotate wbq-drain spawn failures with the worker name and the returned
  std::io::Error.
- Annotate commit-coalescer worker spawn failures with the worker index
  and the returned std::io::Error.

This keeps the existing fail-fast behavior while making the failure mode
operationally debuggable instead of opaque.
